nits: Migration backfill not strictly idempotent on a partially-populated re-run: any orphan composio_poll_state row (automation_id not in automations) would leave workspace_id NULL, causing SET NOT NULL to fail. Currently safe (table empty + automation_id FK has ON DELETE CASCADE preventing orphans), but a defensive guard would harden against manual schema-history replays.
Spec said file would be 0028_h3_poll_state_workspace_id.sql but landed as 0030_* because slots 0028/0029 were taken (G.2 + G.4). Journal idx 30 is consistent; just a doc-vs-impl drift worth noting.
F.9 INSERT now writes workspace_id, gaining a workspaces FK with ON DELETE CASCADE. Additive to the existing automation_id FK CASCADE — automations CASCADE would have removed these rows anyway.
SQL-shape test asserts the fairness substring but doesn't behaviorally exercise the partition — accepted because H.7 live load test is the definitive proof.
POLL_PER_WORKSPACE_CAP env override is read via Number(env) every sweep; NaN (empty string) would silently produce r.rn <= NaN → zero rows. Consider Number.isFinite guard with fallback to 5.
reviewer: opus-4.7 adversarial pass via Agent tool
